title: Remove CUDA-exclusive third-party imports (apex, apex_C) that have no NPU equivalent
problem_description: Phase 4 rule migration produced an empty operation set, failing to address a deliberately broken `from apex_C import fused_layer_norm` import. Apex is a CUDA-exclusive NVIDIA library with no NPU equivalent. The script failed with ModuleNotFoundError on first run, requiring the Phase 5 repair agent to identify and remove the import along with all other CUDA→NPU patterns in a single repair step.
rough_fix_approach: 1. Scan entry script and all project source files for imports of CUDA-exclusive packages: apex, apex_C, apex.parallel, apex.amp, pyximport with CUDA, cuda_ext, etc. 2. For each CUDA-exclusive import, determine if it has an NPU equivalent (most do not) and remove it entirely. 3. Verify the import was not used downstream — if referenced in code, also remove or replace those call sites. 4. Run the script and confirm no further import errors remain.
artifact_evidence: raw/phase_5_validation_attempt0.json:line 63 (last_error_signature with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'apex_C')
validated/phase_4_rule_migration_canonical.json:line 2 (empty operation set proving rule migration missed this)

involved_code_files.role: Entry script — contained CUDA-exclusive apex import at line 25 (pre-repair), line 3 (post-repair adds import torch_npu)

reasoning: CUDA-exclusive third-party libraries (Apex, NVIDIA's CUDA extensions, cutlass-based ops, FlashAttention v1, etc.) are common in real-world PyTorch projects. Simple rule-based text replacement cannot detect or remove these imports because they are not torch.cuda.* patterns. A dedicated skill for identifying and removing CUDA-exclusive dependencies generalizes to virtually all CUDA→NPU migrations.
